title: ECharts dashboard showcase (every primitive)
description: Canonical proof-of-coverage demo. Every primitive in dashboards_hub.md catalog index appears here, MECE-grouped across eight tabs.

summary.title: What this dashboard exercises
summary.body:
Eight MECE tabs covering every primitive in `dashboards_hub.md` -- 30 chart types, 10 widget kinds, 10 filter types, 11 filter ops, 6 note kinds, 5 annotation types, 8 KPI aggregators, 11 table formats, 4 tool input kinds, 8 tool output kinds, 4 sync modes, 4 brush types, both layouts.

Each tab opens with a markdown header naming the primitives demonstrated; every chart / table / KPI carries a per-widget subtitle calling out the specific primitive it exercises. **This is the proof-of-coverage demo** -- if any tab fails to render, the engine has a regression.

metadata.methodology:
## Sources

All synthetic, deterministic (SEED-controlled in `dev/demos.py`). The data shapes mirror what PRISM emits from production data sources -- enough to exercise every primitive without depending on live pulls.

## Construction

* Tab 1-3 use `pull_rates_panel`, `pull_cross_asset`, `_pull_macro_panel`, `_pull_global_*`, `_pull_portfolio_*`, `_pull_fomc_*`, `_pull_equity_*` from `dev/demos.py`.
* Tab 4-6 add small inline DataFrames sized to each primitive's minimum demonstration shape.
* Tab 7 reads canonical tool defs from `dev/tool_examples/<name>/`.
* Tab 8 runs `manifest_template`, `populate_template`, `validate_manifest`, `chart_data_diagnostics` against deliberately-imperfect manifests at compile time, embeds the live output into markdown widgets.

## What's NOT exercised here

* Live data pulls (no network).
* The persistence + refresh build flow (Tools 1+2+3 from `dashboards_hub.md` Section 6) -- tested in PRISM.
* The `Refresh` button (`metadata.refresh_frequency` set to `manual`).
* Vision-QC (deprecated; see `prism/vision-qc.md`).

rows.content:
## Tab 1 -- Time series & comparison

- `line`
- `multi_line`
- `bar`
- `bar_horizontal`
- `area`
- `candlestick`
- `slope`
- `annotations: hline / vline / band / arrow / point`
- `mapping.axes (multi-axis)`
- `strokeDash`
- `y_log`
- `per-chart dataZoom`
- `stat_strip (auto)`

Read the upper-left UST levels chart first: it carries every annotation type plus the auto stat strip. The dual-axis tile below it routes four series (SPX / UST / DXY / WTI) onto four independent axes via `mapping.axes`. The right column cycles through `bar` / `bar_horizontal` / `area` / `slope` -- all share the same `gs_primary` palette declared on the manifest, no per-chart overrides.

rows.title: UST curve levels (line + annotations)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=multi_line` with all five annotation types: `hline` / `vline` / `band` / `arrow` / `point`. Auto stat strip via the Sigma button.

rows.title: 2s10s spread (line + zero hline)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=line` -- the simplest single-series case. `dateZoom slider` injected automatically.

rows.title: EPS actual vs estimate (bar, grouped)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=bar` with `mapping.color` long-form and `stack=False`. Vertical bars; categorical x.

rows.title: Analyst ratings (bar_horizontal)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=bar_horizontal` -- value on x, category on y. Useful for ranked counts / ratings / analyst tallies.

rows.title: Cumulative P&L by bucket (area, stacked)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=area` -- stacked area on long-form data via `mapping.color`. 4 buckets stays under the line-shaped y-series cap (charts.md §3.1).

rows.title: Sector returns: month-end vs latest (slope)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=slope` -- exactly two snapshots in the x column; right-edge labels per category.

rows.title: Equity OHLC (candlestick)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=candlestick` -- requires `open` / `close` / `low` / `high` mapping. Builders managing their own dataZoom are left alone.

rows.title: Cross-asset overlay (mapping.axes, log scale)
rows.subtitle: Four series, four independent axes via `mapping.axes`: SPX (left), UST (right inverted), DXY (left offset), WTI (right offset). `y_log` on SPX axis.

rows.content:
## Tab 2 -- Distribution & relationship

- `scatter (with trendline)`
- `scatter_multi (per-group OLS)`
- `scatter_studio`
- `histogram`
- `boxplot`
- `bullet`
- `correlation_matrix (runtime drawer)`
- `heatmap`
- `calendar_heatmap`

`scatter_studio` (row 2, full-width) is the most interactive primitive in the catalog: viewer picks X / Y / color / size / per-axis transform / regression off the drawer; stats strip recomputes on the fly. `correlation_matrix` (row 4 left) carries its own runtime drawer for transform / window / method without re-querying PRISM.

rows.title: Beta scatter (with trendline)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=scatter` + `trendline: True` adds an overall OLS line + R^2.

rows.title: Factor returns vs market (scatter_multi, OLS per group)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=scatter_multi` + per-group OLS via `trendlines: True`. One regression line per color.

rows.title: Macro panel -- scatter_studio (interactive)
rows.subtitle: Pick X / Y / color / size + per-axis transform from the controls drawer. Stats strip recomputes on every change.

rows.title: Daily returns (histogram, density)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=histogram`, `bins=24`, `density=True`.

rows.title: Factor returns by sector (boxplot)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=boxplot` -- categorical x, numeric y. Whiskers + outliers auto-computed.

rows.title: Valuation vs 5Y range (bullet)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=bullet` -- current marker against a low/high band per category.

rows.title: Cross-asset correlations (correlation_matrix)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=correlation_matrix` -- columns auto-compute pairwise corr; runtime drawer flips transform / window / method without reload.

rows.title: Sector x month returns (heatmap)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=heatmap` -- pre-computed bivariate cells via `(x, y, value)`. `gs_diverging` palette.

rows.title: Daily returns calendar (calendar_heatmap)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=calendar_heatmap` -- one cell per calendar day; `(date, value)` mapping; `gs_diverging` palette.

tabs.label: 3. Hierarchy & flow
tabs.description: Hierarchical, flow, and specialty chart types.

rows.content:
## Tab 3 -- Hierarchy, flow & specialty

- `pie`
- `donut`
- `treemap`
- `sunburst`
- `funnel`
- `sankey`
- `graph`
- `tree`
- `parallel_coords`
- `radar`
- `gauge`
- `waterfall`
- `fan_cone`
- `marimekko`

Charts here intentionally have no `dataZoom` slider -- none of these types have a time axis. The `treemap` and `sunburst` share the same hierarchical dataset to show the two views of the same shape.

rows.title: Allocation (pie)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=pie` -- (category, value).

rows.title: Outbound trade share (donut)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=donut` -- pie with hole.

rows.title: VaR utilisation (gauge)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=gauge` -- `value` against `min` / `max` arc.

rows.title: AUM treemap
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=treemap` -- `path` + `value`. Click to drill, breadcrumb to ascend.

rows.title: AUM sunburst
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=sunburst` -- same data + same `path` mapping; radial layout.

rows.title: Mandate funnel
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=funnel` -- ordered conversion stages with descending magnitudes.

rows.title: Bilateral trade flows (sankey)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=sankey` -- (source, target, value).

rows.title: Counterparty network (graph)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=graph` -- adds `node_category` for auto-coloured clusters. Drag nodes.

rows.title: Counterparty hierarchy (tree)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=tree` -- (name, parent). Click a node to collapse subtree.

rows.title: Position snapshot (parallel_coords)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=parallel_coords` -- multi-dim per-row visualisation. `color` highlights buckets.

rows.title: Factor exposure (radar)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=radar` -- (category, value, series). Series overlap on shared axes.

rows.title: P&L bridge (waterfall)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=waterfall` -- `is_total` flags open / close as full-height bars.

rows.title: Rate-path forecast (fan_cone)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=fan_cone` -- central path + N stacked confidence ribbons.

rows.title: Cap-weighted sector x size (marimekko)
rows.subtitle: `chart_type=marimekko` -- 2D categorical proportions. x-col widths = column shares; y stacks proportionally inside each column.

tabs.label: 4. Tables & KPIs
tabs.description: All 8 KPI aggregators, all 11 table formats, pivot.

rows.content:
## Tab 4 -- Tabular widgets

- `kpi (8 aggregators: latest / first / sum / mean / min / max / count / prev)`
- `stat_grid`
- `table (11 formats, in_cell bar + sparkline, conditional formatting, color_scale, row_click rich drilldown)`
- `pivot`

Each KPI tile in row 1 names the aggregator it pulls (`<dataset>.<agg>.<column>` syntax). The rich table in row 3 has one column for every `format` value in the spec; click any row to open a multi-section drilldown modal.

rows.label: Sum of 2s10s prints
rows.info: aggregator: `sum` -- bp prints summed across the window. Demonstrative only.

rows.title: Risk summary (stat_grid)
rows.subtitle: Dense grid of label / value stats; takes less vertical space than a KPI ribbon.

stats.label: Beta to SPX
stats.info: OLS beta of book P&L vs S&P 500 TR, trailing 60 biz days.

rows.title: Rich table -- all 11 formats + in-cell bar + sparkline
rows.subtitle:
| `format` | renders |
|---|---|
| `text` | string |
| `number` | 12345.67 |
| `integer` | 12345 |
| `percent` | 0.123 -> 12.30% |
| `currency` | $12,345.67 |
| `bps` | +25 bp |
| `date` | 2026-04-22 |
| `datetime` | 2026-04-22 16:00 |
| `link` | github.com -> clickable |
| `signed` | +12.34 / -5.67 |
| `delta` | Δ +0.25 |

rows.title: Sector x window perf (pivot)
rows.subtitle: Long-form `(sector, country, ticker, window, ret)`. User picks row dim / col dim / value / agg from the dropdowns; cells use the `gs_diverging` color scale.

tabs.label: 5. Prose & header
tabs.description: Note kinds + markdown grammar + image + divider.

rows.content:
## Tab 5 -- Prose, media & header

- `note (6 kinds: insight / thesis / watch / risk / context / fact)`
- `markdown (full grammar)`
- `image`
- `divider`
- `metadata.summary (always-on banner above row 1)`
- `metadata.methodology (header chrome popup)`
- `header_actions (custom buttons left of chrome)`

The summary banner / methodology popup / header_actions live on the manifest's metadata + header_actions blocks (visible at the top of every tab, not on this tab specifically). The 6 note kinds below are paired so each kind sits next to its near-mate.

rows.title: Insight
rows.content:
The 2s10s curve has been drifting flatter for **six consecutive sessions** -- the persistence is the signal, not the magnitude.

Front end is still pricing ~3 cuts; the long end has barely moved.

rows.title: Thesis
rows.content:
Carry-and-roll on belly USTs continues to outperform either bull-flatten or bear-steepen trades on a Sharpe basis until the **next FOMC**.

Long 5s30s steepener via 1Y rec / receivers stays the cleanest expression.

rows.title: Watch
rows.content:
**Levels to watch:**

- 10Y above 4.55% reopens the bear-steepen trade
- 2s10s back to flat -> structural bid for credit
- VIX > 22 invalidates the carry trade

rows.title: Risk
rows.content:
**Pain trade is a hot CPI print Friday.**

Front end re-prices in 24 hours, breakevens widen, the carry trade unwinds in a single session. Stop on a 12bp reversal.

rows.title: Context
rows.content: Cycle is in the **mid-cut phase**. Three cuts delivered (Sep / Nov / Dec); next FOMC is in 12 sessions. SLOOS turned net easing in February. Earnings revisions positive.

rows.title: Fact
rows.content:
**Point-in-time facts (as of close):**

- Fed funds target: 4.00 - 4.25%
- Last FOMC: 2026-03-12 (cut 25bp)
- Next FOMC: 2026-05-07 (12 sessions)
- Last NFP: +152k vs +180k cons

## Markdown grammar (shared across all prose surfaces)

Same grammar applies to: `widget: markdown`, `metadata.summary`, `metadata.methodology`, every `popup: {body}`, table `row_click` markdown sections.

### Inline
**bold**, *italic*, ~~strike~~, `code`, [link](https://example.com).

### Block
Lists:

- bullet
- another bullet
  - nested (2-space indent)

1. ordered
2. ordered (numbers don't have to be sequential)

### Blockquote

> The most reliable signal is when the curve and the credit market disagree about the same recession.

### Code block

```python
manifest = {'datasets': {'rates': df_rates}, 'layout': {...}}
compile_dashboard(manifest, strict=True)
```

### Table

| Surface | Markdown? |
|---|---|
| `widget: markdown` | Yes |
| `metadata.summary` | Yes |
| Tab `description` | No (plain text) |
| Tab `label` | No (plain text) |

### Horizontal rule

---

Anything not matching is escaped as plain text -- including raw HTML.

rows.title: image widget
rows.subtitle: `widget: image` -- src URL or static path. Embeds with `alt` + optional `link`.

rows.title: Image widget usage
rows.content: Use `widget: image` for: section banners (left edge of `markets_wrap`), small visual callouts, vendor logos in the header, sparkline-shaped explainers between data rows. The renderer treats `src` as opaque -- HTTP URLs, static `/static/img/...` paths, or base64 data URIs all work.

tabs.label: 6. Filters & sync
tabs.description: Every filter type, sync, brush, click-emit, click-popup.

rows.content:
## Tab 6 -- Filters & cross-sync

- `10 filter types: dateRange / select / multiSelect / numberRange / toggle / slider / radio / text / number / rule`
- `11 filter ops: == / != / > / >= / < / <= / contains / startsWith / endsWith / in / not_in`
- `cascading filters (depends_on + options_from)`
- `links sync (axis / tooltip / legend / dataZoom)`
- `links brush (rect / polygon / lineX / lineY)`
- `click_emit_filter`
- `click_popup`
- `row_click`

All filters on this tab scope to `tab:filters` so they render inline above the widgets. The donut on row 4 has `click_emit_filter` wired to the bar chart beside it; click a slice to filter. Row 5 has a scatter with `click_popup` -- click any point for a multi-section drilldown. The two line charts in row 3 share a `Link` with `sync: ['axis', 'tooltip', 'dataZoom']` plus a `brush: lineX`.

rows.title: Cascading filters: region -> country
rows.subtitle: Pick a region; the country dropdown rebuilds. Two `select` filters wired via `depends_on` + `options_from`.

rows.title: Universe (every filter type targets this)
rows.subtitle: Apply any flat filter or the rule filter; rows trim. Multi-filter AND.

rows.title: SPX (sync member)
rows.subtitle: Linked to fil_sync_b via `sync: ['axis', 'tooltip', 'dataZoom']` + `brush: lineX`. Drag a slider on either chart.

rows.title: Gold (sync member)
rows.subtitle: Same Link as fil_sync_a; tooltip + axis + zoom track.

rows.title: Outbound region (click_emit_filter -> region)
rows.subtitle: Click a slice; the bar chart on the right filters to that region via `click_emit_filter: 'region_filter'`.

rows.title: Sector returns (filtered by region)
rows.subtitle: Auto-filters to the selected region from the donut click.

rows.title: Clickable scatter (click_popup -> rich modal)
rows.subtitle: Each point opens a multi-section drilldown modal: stats / markdown / chart / table. Same grammar as table `row_click`.

tabs.label: 7. Tools
tabs.description: widget: tool -- inline def, JS compute, all kinds.

rows.content:
## Tab 7 -- Tool widgets

- `widget: tool (form-driven compute)`
- `input kinds: scalar / sweep / expression / matrix (scalar covers number / range / date / text / select / radio / toggle)`
- `output kinds: stat / scalar / param / kpi / series / table / stat_grid / distribution`
- `inline tool_def (no registry; def + JS travel with manifest)`

Three canonical tool patterns. Bond pricer (scalars + radio toggle + table output). Black-Scholes (range sliders for vol + sweep width, stat headline + series chart with `x_from: input.K`). Fed scenarios (scalar dates + 24x3 matrix input + series + table outputs).

rows.title: Fixed-coupon bond pricer (scalars + table)
rows.subtitle: Scalar inputs (price, coupon, maturity) + a radio mode toggle (Price->YTM vs YTM->Price). Outputs: stat headline + cashflow `table`.

tool_def.name: bond_pricer
tool_def.title: Fixed-coupon bond pricer
tool_def.description: Price a fixed-coupon bullet bond. Input price OR yield; the other is solved.

rows.title: Black-Scholes option (stat headline + series)
rows.subtitle: Greeks reported in trader units. P&L vs spot `series` chart with a `vline` annotation that tracks `input.K` live.

tool_def.name: option_bsm
tool_def.title: Black-Scholes option pricer
tool_def.description: European call/put price + greeks. Optional spot sweep for P&L curve.

rows.title: Fed scenario swap rates (matrix input + series)
rows.subtitle: 24-row x 3-col `matrix` input (paste from Excel supported) + scalar dates + scalar starting FF. Outputs: swap-rate `table` + FF-path `series`.

tool_def.name: fed_scenario_swaps
tool_def.title: Fed scenario swap rates
tool_def.description: Compute OIS-style spot and forward swap rates under multiple FOMC hike/cut scenarios.

rows.content:
## Tab 8 -- Dev workflow & diagnostics

- `manifest_template + populate_template`
- `validate_manifest`
- `chart_data_diagnostics`
- `strict=True / strict=False`
- `save_pngs`
- `chart variants: spec / option / ref`
- `raw chart_type`
- `computed datasets`
- `field_provenance`
- `show_when`
- `initial_state`
- `stat_strip`

The four markdown panels in row 1 carry **live output** from helper calls run at compile time -- not mocked text. Row 2 demonstrates the three chart variants on the same data. Row 3 shows a chart driven by a manifest-level `compute` block with `system: 'computed'` provenance auto-stamped.

## `manifest_template` -> `populate_template`

**Step 1** -- `manifest_template(seed)` strips data rows from every dataset, leaving only the header row. The template's `datasets.rates.source` now holds 1 row with columns: `['date', 'us_2y', 'us_5y', 'us_10y', 'us_30y', '2s10s', '5s30s', '10y_real']`.

Templates are pure JSON -- safe to persist, diff in source control, hand-edit, and re-feed each refresh.

**Step 2** -- `populate_template(template, {'rates': df})` re-injects fresh data. The populated `datasets.rates.source` now holds **252 data rows** (plus header); values are JSON-serialisable scalars. Pass `require_all_slots=True` to fail loudly when the template declares a dataset slot the call doesn't fill.

## `validate_manifest`

Status: **FAILED** -- 5 error(s) on a deliberately-broken manifest.

Cheapest pre-compile pass: structure / types / id uniqueness / cross-references / required keys.

| # | Path / message |
|---|---|
| 1 | `filters[0].options: required for type 'select'` |
| 2 | `filters[1].id: duplicate id 'x'` |
| 3 | `layout.rows[0][0].spec.dataset: 'missing_dataset' not declared in manifest.datasets (available: ['rates'])` |
| 4 | `layout.rows[0][1].id: duplicate widget id 'c'` |
| 5 | `layout.rows[0][1].spec.chart_type: 'frobnicator' not in ['area', 'bar', 'bar_horizontal', 'boxplot', 'bullet', 'calendar_heatmap', 'candlestick', 'correlation_matrix', 'donut', 'fan_cone', 'funnel', 'gauge', 'graph', 'heatmap', 'histogram', 'line', 'marimekko', 'multi_line', 'parallel_coords', 'pie', 'radar', 'sankey', 'scatter', 'scatter_multi', 'scatter_studio', 'slope', 'sunburst', 'tree', 'treemap', 'waterfall']` |

## `chart_data_diagnostics`

Returned **3** diagnostic(s) on a 4-widget manifest with one healthy chart + an empty dataset + a missing column + a KPI source typo.

Diagnostic codes are stable -- pattern-match on `code` to drive automated repair.

| Severity | Code | Widget | Message |
|---|---|---|---|
| error | `chart_dataset_empty` | `empty_chart` | chart 'empty_chart' references dataset 'empty' which has 0 rows; chart will render blank. |
| error | `chart_mapping_column_missing` | `missing_col` | mapping.y='us_99y' is not a column in dataset 'rates'. |
| error | `kpi_source_column_missing` | `k_typo` | kpi 'k_typo' source='rates.latest.us_99y' references column 'us_99y' which is not in dataset 'rates'. |

rows.title: Variant 1: spec
rows.subtitle: `{chart_type, dataset, mapping}` -- the PRISM-preferred path.

rows.title: Variant 2: option (raw passthrough)
rows.subtitle: Hand-rolled ECharts option dict on the widget; bypasses the builder.

rows.title: Variant 3: ref
rows.subtitle: `ref: 'echarts/<file>.json'` -- spec loaded from a sibling JSON file. Compiler resolves paths relative to the manifest's parent dir.

rows.title: Computed dataset: 2s10s spread (bp)
rows.subtitle: `spreads.compute.us_2s10s_bp = (us_10y - us_2y) * 100`; provenance auto-stamps `system: 'computed'` + the recipe string.

rows.title: initial_state: opens in YoY %
rows.subtitle: Same data as the levels chart on Tab 1; `spec.initial_state.transform = 'yoy_pct'` opens the controls drawer pre-set to YoY %.
